title: trollius altissimus subsp. tatrae
intro: Trollius altissimus subsp. tatrae, also known as the Tatra Globe Flower, is a rare and endangered subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Trollius altissimus subsp. tatrae include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its possible antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Trollius altissimus subsp. tatrae can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal dosages.
